primary_factors: No accumulating snow during the 5–9 AM bus/commute window (trace/dusting at most, if any)
Wind chills around ~20°F at bus time—cold but far above Michigan closure thresholds (no ≤-20°F trigger from any verified source)

Main risk is *patchy refreeze/black ice* from Wed rain/freezing-rain flags + Thu dawn temps in the upper 20s; typically treatable and more likely to cause isolated slick spots or a delay than a full closure
No confirmed ‘domino effect’: no verified neighboring district closure/delay announcements found; little/no community buzz
Power outages in Kent County appear low and decreasing in the latest snapshot; unlikely to drive closure absent a specific school-building outage
decision_rationale: All experts converged on an ‘open’ meteorological pattern for Thu 3/12 in Rockford: essentially no meaningful snow during the morning commute, good visibility, and no extreme-cold trigger (feels-like ~20°F, not dangerous by Michigan closure rules). The only plausible closure pathway is operational rather than meteorological: patchy but potentially impactful black ice from antecedent wet/freezing-rain conditions on Wed evening followed by clear, subfreezing pre-dawn temps Thu. That risk is real, but historically it produces isolated slick spots and, at most, a 2-hour delay if early route checks find widespread glazing—especially on secondary/rural roads and bus turnarounds. News intel found no official Rockford or neighboring district closure/delay signals and no verified Kent-specific widespread-ice incident chatter. Power-grid risk is low with outages trending downward in the latest county snapshot. Net: a full snow-day closure is unlikely; the more realistic disruption is a delay if 5–6 AM road checks come back poor.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 2.0% spread. Average probability: 7%.
